transcript: of all the stress in your life. Over the course of a day a number of stressful events can accumulate in your stress bucket causing your stress levels to rise. You might have an assessment coming up, be worried about money or be in conflict with family or friends. If you have no way of handling all this built-up stress you may feel overwhelmed, angry or unable to cope. Your stress bucket begins to overflow. And worse still, research has found prolonged exposure to everyday stress can make you more susceptible to anxiety and depression, weight gain, heart disease and it can even weaken your immune system. In times of stress it can be tempting to turn to quick fixes like consuming alcohol drugs comfort eating or avoiding the problem while this may provide short-term relief the stress still remains often making you more stressed as a result it's not all bad news though as there are proven strategies that you can use to lower your stress level mindfulness and relaxation techniques will not only help you handle your stress but will also improve your memory and recall try breathing deeply and slowly which will calm your body and mind exercise go for a walk and get your body moving this releases endorphins boosting your mood and motivation do something you love like watching a movie listening to music or catching up with friends learn to control your time and prioritize focus on what needs to be done first and be kind to yourself eat healthy food sleep for at least seven hours a night and take it one step at a time finally get some help you don't have to manage your stress on your own so get help from a counselor or friends all these strategies are proven ways to reduce stress stress is a part of life and while you can't control the stressful situations that come into your life you can control how you respond to and manage them. That bit is up to you.
